findings.description: App.tsx still renders only <Board issues={issues} /> and never mounts SyncPointBanner. This is the same defect flagged as round-1 codex #1 and is unaddressed. Per proposal section 3 ('Sync-point gate banner pinned to the top of the board') the banner is one of the three MVP surfaces. Fix: in App.tsx, render <SyncPointBanner apiUrl={API_URL} apiKey={API_KEY} /> above <Board>; thread the configured URL/key through.

Card.tsx does not import or render VendorSwimlanes for in-flight cards. The component exists with a proper AgentActivity contract, has tests in __tests__/VendorSwimlanes.test.tsx, but is unreachable from the rendered board. Round-1 codex #2 is unaddressed. Fix: in Card.tsx, when issue.status is in {claimed, running}, render <VendorSwimlanes agents={...} completed={false} /> using child-agent data sourced from the snapshot/audit stream; extend useCoordinator to project per-issue agent activity.

useCoordinator's fetchIssues still sends labels: ['change:a','change:b'] as a single array. IssueService.list_issues treats this as ALL-of (AND) semantics, so multi-change boards return zero issues. Round-1 codex #5 unaddressed. Fix: either iterate changeIds and merge results (Promise.all + dedupe by id), or add a coordinator endpoint with explicit OR semantics. Mirror the fix in event_stream._build_snapshot which uses the same flat-list pattern (line 170).

Double state update in the transition SSE handler: setIssues((current) => { applyTransition(payload, current); return current.map(...) }) — applyTransition itself calls setIssues internally (line 104), so each transition triggers two state updates with the same payload, causing redundant renders and a subtle race when transitions arrive faster than React batches. Round-1 gemini #2 unaddressed. Fix: drop the applyTransition(payload, current) call inside the reducer; keep only the pure map; remove the applyTransition useCallback or repurpose it to return the new array.

Kick flow is broken end-to-end. sync_points.get_sync_points_status() emits blocker rows with only agent_id and last_heartbeat_iso — no change_id (lines 106-112). SyncPointBanner.tsx line 208 then passes the literal string 'unknown' as change_id to /agents/{agent_id}/kick. The kick endpoint passes that to worktree.py teardown unknown --agent-id <agent_id> --force, which cannot match the real registry entry. Round-1 codex #6 and gemini #1 unaddressed. Fix: (a) include change_id in each blocker dict in sync_points.py (registry entries carry it); (b) thread change_id through SyncPointBanner.handleKickClick instead of 'unknown'; (c) add an integration test that drives a real blocker and asserts non-default change_id reaches the registry teardown.

Audit SSE events are dropped for any audit row whose CoordinatorEvent.change_id is unset, because _on_audit_event short-circuits when not evt.change_id. The existing coordinator_audit pathway does not populate change_id on every event; this means vendor swimlanes can never reflect live activity. Round-1 codex #3 unaddressed. Fix: either derive change_id from agent_id (split on '--') before filtering, or have the audit producer stamp change_id, or relax the filter to match agent_id prefixes. Add a test that exercises an audit event without change_id and asserts it reaches a subscribed connection.

Transition `from` field can emit empty string, violating the SSE schema enum. _make_transition uses ctx.get('from_status') or ctx.get('from', '') and ctx.get('to_status') or ctx.get('to', evt.event_type.split('.')[-1]); if the upstream notify payload lacks prior status, 'from' becomes '' which is not in the enum {pending,claimed,running,completed,failed}. Round-1 codex #4 unaddressed. Fix: ensure the LISTEN/NOTIFY trigger always emits old_status; if absent, drop the event rather than emit an invalid one. Also verify the trigger covers claimed→running transitions — the proposal requires live running updates and the audit notes the existing trigger only fires for completed/failed/claimed.

kanban_viz_files.write_saved_view and write_audit_event stamp the document and atomic-write without validating the supplied view_payload/event_payload against contracts/schemas/*. Garbage in, committed garbage out — any bad UI write becomes a git diff. Round-1 codex #7 unaddressed. Fix: load the corresponding JSON schema once at module import (saved-view.schema.json, audit-event.schema.json), call jsonschema.validate(document) AFTER stamping and BEFORE _atomic_write, and have the FastAPI endpoint return 400 with the validation error path on failure.

The /audit extension landed as a separate route /audit/v2 (line 3030) instead of being added to the existing GET /audit handler (line 1223). proposal §3 explicitly states 'extend GET /audit with since and change_id for polling fallback'. The forked route also means useCoordinator's polling-fallback path never uses it (the hook only re-fetches /issues/list, no audit polling at all — see startPolling at line 115). Round-1 codex #8 unaddressed. Fix: merge the v2 handler back into GET /audit (since/change_id are optional, fully backward-compatible), delete /audit/v2, and add an audit-polling branch to useCoordinator.startPolling so swimlanes degrade gracefully when SSE is unavailable.

Token-in-URL hardening required by D2/proposal §3 is still missing. /events/work accepts the JWT as a ?token= query parameter (line 2711), but: (a) there is no access-log redaction of the token query — uvicorn's default access log will record it; (b) the served frontend has no Referrer-Policy: no-referrer header, so a third-party link click leaks the URL+token via Referer; (c) the response itself sets no Cache-Control: no-store to prevent intermediate caching of the URL. Round-1 codex #9 unaddressed. Fix: add a middleware that strips token= from request.url before logging; emit Referrer-Policy: no-referrer and Cache-Control: no-store on /events/work and the static frontend; ideally also accept the token in a custom EventSource polyfill header so the URL form can be deprecated.

sse-starlette is imported at module load by event_stream.py (the SSE generator format is sse-starlette's) but is still absent from pyproject.toml optional-dependencies [api]. PyJWT was added (lines 57 and 67); sse-starlette was not. Round-1 gemini #3 partially addressed. Fix: add 'sse-starlette>=2.0.0' alongside 'PyJWT>=2.8.0' in both the [api] extras and any other extras that should ship the SSE generator. Without it, pip install .[api] in a fresh env will fail at import time.

skills/tests/agent-coordinator/test_kanban_viz_endpoints.py remains as dead duplicate test code outside the coordinator pytest path — same finding as round-1 codex #10 / gemini #4 / OC-1. Two consecutive review rounds have flagged this; remove the file (or move the canonical tests there and prune the agent-coordinator/tests copy). The presence of two test files with similar names is actively misleading for future reviewers and IDE search.

kick_agent always invokes worktree.py teardown <change_id> --agent-id <agent_id>, but the registry can contain entries where agent_id is null (single-agent worktrees keyed on change_id alone). In that case worktree.py refuses to match and returns REMOVED=false, so the kick reports failure even when there was a legitimate single-agent blocker. Fix: in kick_agent, first look up the registry entry by agent_id (and by change_id when agent_id is the change-scoped key) and only pass --agent-id when the registry actually carries one; alternatively, support a 'change-scoped' teardown form in worktree.py and choose between them. Add a test for the single-agent (agent_id is null) blocker case.

The audit SSE callback parses the payload but then explicitly drops the result (es.addEventListener('audit', ...) sets a const _payload and immediately voids it at line 178). The audit channel is therefore observable only in dev-tools — no state update, no logging, no per-card surface. This means even after fixing finding #2 (mount VendorSwimlanes), there is still no path for audit data to reach a card's swimlane. Fix: extend useCoordinator's return type with a per-issue or per-agent activity map populated from audit events; thread it through Board → Column → Card → VendorSwimlanes.

Docstring in coordination_api.py for GET /sync-points/status (around line 2654) claims to reuse skills.shared.active_agents, but D5 in sync_points.py explicitly duplicates the logic for Docker isolation. Round-1 gemini #5 unaddressed. Fix: update the docstring to state 'reads .git-worktrees/.registry.json directly (D5) to avoid skills/ import in the deployed image' so future readers don't try to refactor toward the imported helper.
